message:
feat(core): quotient filter scaffolding for DELETE/UPDATE short-circuit (IMPL-9a partial)

Introduce the per-table QuotientFilter bookkeeping + helpers needed to wire
absent-rowid short-circuit into the direct-simple DELETE/UPDATE fast path
(landed as T1.3 / IMPL-1 in 737d7a45). This commit is scaffolding only —
the helpers compile clean and carry `#[allow(dead_code)]` until the
consumption sites (direct-simple DELETE/UPDATE entries, INSERT post-commit
hook, rollback invalidation, DDL invalidation) are wired in a follow-up.

Added primitives on `Connection`:
- `QuotientFilterEntry { filter, built, too_large_to_build, modified_in_txn }`
- `quotient_filters: RefCell<HashMap<i32, QuotientFilterEntry>>`
- `quotient_filter_short_circuits: Cell<u64>` observability counter
- `quotient_filter_seed_from_cursor` (build-on-first-consult, with a
  `QUOTIENT_FILTER_BUILD_ROW_CAP = 10M` cap to skip too-large tables)
- `qf_maybe_short_circuit_for_rowid` (consultation entry point)
- `qf_record_insert` / `qf_record_delete` (maintenance)
- `invalidate_qfs_on_rollback` + `clear_qf_txn_modifications` (txn boundary)
- `qf_invalidate_root` (DDL invalidation)

Strategy (design notes for the follow-up wiring):
- Per-connection filter storage (option b in the design memo), not
  shared via schema Arc — simpler lifecycle, ~24 MiB/table/conn.
- Build-on-first-consult: filter starts unbuilt; first DELETE/UPDATE
  consultation triggers a one-shot scan to seed it.
- Invalidate-on-rollback: any filter with `modified_in_txn=true` is
  reset; next consultation reseeds from the committed B-tree.
- False-negatives are FATAL (would under-delete); only consult after
  `built=true` and invalidate on rollback/DDL so a never-committed
  insert can never say "present" to the caller.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
